world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 18
major_event: The Dur Khadur recall dispatch reached the unit commander at midday. He read it twice, sent a confirmation query back to the Dark Council, and told his men to rest but not to strike camp. The unit remains at four miles. The dispatch has been received but not yet obeyed.

primary_event.name: The Recall Arrives — Unit Holds
primary_event.summary: The Tidefall recall dispatch reached the Dur Khadur unit commander at midday on Day 18. He read it, sent a confirmation query to the Dark Council, and ordered his men to rest but not to strike camp. The Dark Council has not yet replied. The unit is in a state of formal receipt but non-compliance. Caeris does not yet know the dispatch arrived. He sent his second Hollow Accord letter north this morning and waits.

supporting_events.name: Elder Ashveil — One Day Out, Still Blind
supporting_events.summary: Elder Ashveil camped overnight in the foothills north of Stonebreak. She is now one day from Lostfeld's outer gate. Her two druid wardens are experienced but Ashveil has no intelligence on Varek's condition — the Monastery's bid for the Vilefin package failed. She rides with authority but without knowledge of what she is walking into.

supporting_events.name: Rowen — Improvised Gear Ready, First Descent Tomorrow
supporting_events.summary: The ship's carpenter produced weighted descent lines by evening. An old sailor named Briggs claimed deep-water experience off the Dreadwind cliffs. Sealed lanterns were improvised from cook-oil and tin fittings. Rowen has set the dive for dawn. He has told no one what the iron key is for. The crew knows only that the captain found something in the bronze tube worth risking their lives for.

supporting_events.name: Shadow Court Clan Council — One Day Out, Velorn Prepares
supporting_events.summary: Velorn's bloc of five houses finalized its position statement overnight. Arkenor's lieutenant confirmed informal attendance. Lythara's recalled Nythariel envoy has not yet arrived — the Shadow Road takes a full day. She will face the council without the Tidefall intelligence she sent for. Velorn knows this.

active_events.name: Varek and the Iron Case — Sealed Garrison, Day 3
active_events.summary: Third day. Varek has not slept in three days. His breathing is audible in the silence of the sealed room. The iron case shows no external change. At midmorning Varek spoke unprompted: 'Something is listening from below. It has been listening since before the first seal.' Ironmaul did not respond. He left and did not return until evening. The veteran soldiers nearest the case have begun leaving small stone tokens on the floor — a Lostfeld miners' gesture meaning: we do not know what is here but we acknowledge it.
active_events.consequences: Varek's unprompted statement is the first time he has volunteered information. It implies awareness, possibly contact, from whatever is in or near the case. Ironmaul now faces a new pressure: if Varek is in communication with the entity, the Thane is no longer certain which side of the case Varek is on. Ashveil arrives tomorrow.

active_events.name: The Recall Arrives — Unit Holds
active_events.summary: The unit commander received the recall dispatch at midday. He sent a confirmation query to the Dark Council and ordered rest but not withdrawal. The Dark Council's reply will arrive tomorrow at earliest. The camp at four miles remains standing. Caeris has not been informed. His second Hollow Accord letter arrived at Tidefall's admiralty office this morning — it was received by a junior clerk, the Admiral having not yet returned to public function.
active_events.consequences: The unit commander is obeying procedure, not the order. The Dark Council will determine whether compliance serves their interests. If they instruct non-compliance, the situation transforms from diplomatic friction to open defiance. Caeris's Hollow Accord letter now sits in a clerk's inbox, which is its own message about Tidefall's current state of governance.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Exists — Succession Crisis Deepens
active_events.summary: Caeris received no reply to either letter. The Dur Khadur camp persists. He visited Roderic at midmorning — the king did not wake. A palace physician told Caeris privately that cognitive clarity episodes would become less frequent. Caeris returned to his office and did not burn a draft. He wrote nothing. The proclamation is still sealed.
active_events.consequences: The window of wakeful Roderic who might visibly endorse the announcement is narrowing. If Roderic loses the ability to be seen endorsing the succession, Caeris must announce without royal presence — which hands his opponents a procedural grievance. The physician's words function as a final deadline he cannot put a date on.

active_events.name: Elder Ashveil — One Day Out, Still Blind
active_events.summary: Ashveil is one day from Lostfeld's outer gate. She has not received the Vilefin intelligence. She knows Varek is sealed inside and that the instruction rider was turned away twice. She does not know Varek has spoken of an entity listening from below. Her two wardens are armed and authorized. She has composed, mentally, three versions of her opening address to Ironmaul — none of them account for what Varek has become.
active_events.consequences: Tomorrow Ashveil knocks on Ironmaul's gate. She carries override authority the Monastery insists is binding. Ironmaul has informally decided it is not, at least not yet. If the Gnomes sell the Lostfeld intelligence derivative to anyone who can reach Ashveil before dawn, she may arrive better-informed than Ironmaul expects.

active_events.name: Rowen — The Deeper Chamber, Dawn Dive Prepared
active_events.summary: Descent lines are weighted and tested. Briggs has been briefed on depth, direction, and the coordinate. Sealed lanterns are tied to Briggs's belt. Rowen has memorized the vellum strip's markings. The iron key is in Rowen's coat. He will descend alongside Briggs at first light. The crew has been told only: there is a structure below. Return with whatever you find that can be moved.
active_events.consequences: If the dive succeeds, Rowen accesses an sealed chamber no surface explorer has reached. The iron key implies a lock — which implies something was intentionally stored. If the dive fails or the chamber cannot be accessed, Rowen will face the harder choice of returning for proper equipment, which means leaving the site unguarded.

active_events.name: Velorn Consolidation — Shadow Court Clan Council Eve
active_events.summary: The clan council convenes tomorrow. Velorn's five-house bloc has a written position statement. Arkenor's lieutenant is confirmed for informal attendance. Lythara's Nythariel envoy is still in transit — she will not have the Tidefall intelligence before the session opens. Velorn sent a private message to two of Lythara's minor client houses last night: 'She does not know what she does not know. You do not have to follow a queen who reads smoke.'
active_events.consequences: Velorn is attempting to peel Lythara's own client houses before the council session. If even one defects informally, it changes the visible balance in the room. Lythara will enter the council facing a prepared opposition, a fractured intelligence picture, and the knowledge that Velorn is moving faster than she is.

active_events.name: The Admiral's Silence — Internal Tidefall Crisis
active_events.summary: The Admiral did not emerge from his private chamber today. The senior trade councillor spoke this afternoon with two palace staff members who confirmed: no food delivered to the study during the two-day isolation, but a sealed document was slipped under the door on the morning of Day 16 by a figure the staff could not identify. The document has not been described or recovered. The prophet has been seen praying in the hall outside the private chamber door.
active_events.consequences: The sealed document is now the central mystery of the Tidefall crisis. If the council can identify its origin, they may understand what changed the Admiral's posture. The prophet's presence outside the door suggests he believes the Admiral's condition is spiritual rather than political — a belief that will complicate any formal fitness inquiry.

active_events.name: Vilefin Intelligence — Derivatives in Motion
active_events.summary: The Gnomes are moving the derivative sales today. The iron frame and garrison detail package is being packaged for Glenhaven delivery — a Gnome relay runner has departed. The Monastery rider refusal detail is being held for the Shadow Court buyer. The Monastery contact, having lost the bid, has sent a report back to Stonebreak noting the Gnomes now hold the full picture.
active_events.consequences: Glenhaven will receive partial Lostfeld intelligence within one tick. The Shadow Court will receive the Monastery-refusal detail, which confirms Ironmaul's defiance of institutional authority. This detail is exactly what Lythara wants for post-council leverage in the northern trade network argument.

faction_actions.action: The unit commander sent a coded query to the Dark Council: 'Recall dispatch received. Tidefall-origin. Authorization to stand down or hold?' The Dark Council convened a short session. Response: hold until Council confirmation arrives. A return rider departed before afternoon. The unit remains at four miles, tents standing, fires burning low. Cost: 3 gold (courier, unit upkeep), 2 military (continued deployment).
faction_actions.reason: The Dark Council does not take orders from Tidefall directly when a contract's terms are ambiguous. They want to know if the Admiral is acting with full authority or if the recall is a personal deviation from a standing arrangement. Until they know, the unit holds.
faction_actions.target: Unit commander — hold instruction sent; Dark Council — internal deliberation on contract status

faction_actions.action: The Admiral did not emerge. The senior trade councillor convened an informal session of three councillors and shared what the palace staff had told him: a sealed document was delivered under the study door on Day 16. He proposed a formal inquiry motion. The other two councillors declined to sign it yet — too visible, too soon. The junior clerk who received Caeris's Hollow Accord letter left it on the admiralty desk without flagging it urgent. Cost: 1 influence (council tension), 1 gold (administrative operations).
faction_actions.reason: The institution is functioning around the Admiral rather than through him. The councillors are cautious — they do not want to be the faction that formally questioned the Admiral if the recall resolves the crisis cleanly. But the sealed document story is now in circulation among the three of them.
faction_actions.target: Admiral — isolation continues; Caeris Hollow Accord letter — sitting unread on admiralty desk; Council — informal inquiry forming

faction_actions.action: Caeris spent the morning in the archive reviewing the proclamation's exact language. He identified one clause that, if Roderic cannot be publicly present at announcement, grants the regent-designate authority to declare without royal witness — but requires two senior noble countersignatures he does not yet hold. He dispatched a quiet invitation to two loyalist nobles to dine privately tomorrow evening. Cost: 2 gold (archive access, dinner preparation), 2 influence (political navigation).
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is building the announcement's legal scaffolding while the external crises remain unresolved. The two-signature clause is his contingency. He is not ready to use it but he needs it to exist before he needs it urgently.
faction_actions.target: Proclamation legal structure — contingency clause identified; two loyalist nobles — private dinner invitation sent for tomorrow

faction_actions.action: The Monastery contact who lost the Vilefin bid sent a full report to Stonebreak. Grand Druid Varek's superior, Warden Aldric, received it before afternoon and began composing a briefing letter for Ashveil — but the letter cannot reach her before she arrives at Lostfeld's gate. Aldric authorized a Gnome pass-relay message to the nearest northern relay point: 'Ashveil: do not enter without knowing. Entity contact suspected. Proceed with wardens forward.' Whether it reaches her in time is uncertain. Cost: 4 gold (relay message, internal briefing), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Monastery knows they lost the intelligence race. They are trying to correct for it with a relay message, knowing it may arrive too late. Aldric's message uses the phrase 'entity contact suspected' — meaning someone in Stonebreak already suspected Varek's condition was more than physical deterioration.
faction_actions.target: Elder Ashveil — urgent relay message via Gnome pass network; timing uncertain

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul called Kessa Stonebraid to the garrison at midmorning. Kessa — the senior rune-warden of Lostfeld — examined the iron case's outer runes without touching it. She said two things: 'These runes were set to resist opening from within, not from without.' And: 'Something has been pressing against them since before we brought it here.' Ironmaul ordered the garrison doubled and the yard cleared. He sent no word to the outer gate. Cost: 3 gold (garrison doubling, rune-warden consultation), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Varek's statement about something listening from below combined with Kessa's reading has shifted Ironmaul's understanding. The case was not brought to Lostfeld as a prison. It was brought as a seal. And the seal is under pressure from the inside.
faction_actions.target: Kessa Stonebraid — rune examination conducted; garrison — doubled; outer gate — no communication

faction_actions.action: Lythara spent the morning preparing her council address. She has decided on a strategy: rather than defend against Velorn's accusations, she will announce a new intelligence asset — the Tidefall crisis — and frame it as proof that her network's reach exceeds any clan coalition. She will offer the assembled houses a share of whatever leverage emerges from Tidefall's internal fracture. She needs the Nythariel envoy's intelligence to arrive before dawn tomorrow. Cost: 3 gold (Shadow Road communications), 4 influence (council preparation, intelligence cultivation).
faction_actions.reason: Lythara cannot win a defensive argument against a prepared opposition. She is transforming the council session from a referendum on her leadership into a demonstration of her network's superiority. The risk: if the Tidefall intelligence does not arrive in time, she offers a share of leverage she does not yet hold.
faction_actions.target: Clan council — offensive framing prepared; Nythariel envoy — intelligence arrival critical before dawn

faction_actions.action: The Glenhaven derivative package departed via relay runner at dawn. It will reach Glenhaven by tomorrow morning. The Shadow Court derivative — Monastery rider refusal detail — was packaged and left at a dead drop in the eastern Rock Plains that the Court's contact is expected to collect within 24 hours. The Gnomes also authorized their pass-relay station to carry Aldric's urgent message to Ashveil, for a fee of 3 gold paid by the Monastery — the Gnomes logged it, delivered the message north, and noted the Monastery is now reliant on their network in a moment of crisis. Cost: 2 gold (runner, dead drop maintenance), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnomes are running their standard operation: maximize the value extracted from a single intelligence asset, maintain leverage over multiple parties simultaneously, and ensure their pass network remains indispensable to everyone who needs it.
faction_actions.target: Glenhaven — partial Lostfeld intelligence en route; Shadow Court dead drop — Monastery-refusal detail deposited; Monastery — relay message forwarded for fee

faction_actions.action: Elowen reviewed the intelligence purchased from the Gnomes: sealed Lostfeld garrison, unfinished iron frame in the yard, Monastery rider refused twice. She convened a private session with her two senior Wayfarers and issued a precautionary advisory: no Wayfarers to accept passage assignments into Lostfeld territory until further notice. The Wayfarer bearing Glenhaven's facilitation offer is now one day from the Twin Cities. Cost: 2 gold (Wayfarer network advisory, session costs), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Glenhaven's doctrine is non-entanglement until the cost of non-engagement becomes higher than the cost of engagement. The Lostfeld picture is alarming enough to close off exposure. The Twin Cities facilitation offer is still active — that is the play Elowen is watching.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld — Wayfarer access suspended; Twin Cities — facilitation Wayfarer one day out

faction_actions.action: Rowen conducted a final equipment check at sunset. Descent lines: ready. Sealed lanterns: two functional, one questionable. Briggs reviewed the depth estimate. Rowen added himself to the dive — he will descend alongside Briggs rather than wait on deck. The navigator protested; Rowen said: 'The key does not swim by itself.' The crew prepared the descent platform. The iron key was tied to Rowen's wrist with cord. Cost: 2 gold (dive equipment finalization, crew preparation), 1 military (crew safety risk).
faction_actions.reason: Rowen will not send another man to open a door he followed across the sea to find himself. This is consistent with every decision he has made since finding the first document. The navigator's protest is noted but irrelevant.
faction_actions.target: Dawn dive — Rowen and Briggs descending; iron key — secured to Rowen's wrist; descent platform — prepared

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant's false trail held. He reached the midpoint north of the Rock Plains by nightfall and made camp. His 20 gold was divided — 5 buried, 15 carried. He spent the evening composing a mental inventory of what he had seen at Lostfeld that he had not sold: the sound Varek made at night, which was not speech, and the faint smell of cold iron coming from beneath the garrison floor. He has not decided whether these are worth selling. Cost: 1 gold (travel, camp).
faction_actions.reason: The tinker is a professional. He always holds something back. The unsold details are insurance — either against future need or future profit. The smell of cold iron under the floor is something he does not have a framework for yet.
faction_actions.target: Personal cache — gold secured; unsold details — held in reserve; Farrock approach — continuing north

faction_actions.action: Farrock's commander received a vague reply from the Twin Cities: 'Elevated garrison readiness. Foreign activity observed south of city. Situation managed.' He interpreted this as more reassurance than fact. He also received no reply from the Gnome pass relay — the relay is occupied with Monastery traffic today. He ordered a full audit of the northern watchtower signal logs going back seven days. Cost: 2 gold (audit, watchtower operations), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock does not know what is happening but the phrase 'situation managed' from a city that sent an elevated-readiness notice is not reassuring. The watchtower log audit may reveal patterns that the individual signal reports missed.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities response — received and doubted; Gnome relay — no reply yet; northern watchtower logs — audit ordered

faction_actions.action: The junior palace archivist made first contact with the operative today — not by accident. The archivist mentioned, in a casual register, that a sealed document intake had been logged from the admiralty archive three days prior — the same day the Admiral sealed his study. The document's origin was logged as 'external courier, origin unspecified.' The operative relayed this immediately to the Lythara network. Cost: 2 gold (archivist relationship), 2 influence (network relay).
faction_actions.reason: The archivist detail confirms that the sealed document delivered to the Admiral did not originate inside Tidefall. It came from outside. This is the most significant piece of intelligence to emerge from the Tidefall crisis and the Vampires now hold it.
faction_actions.target: Lythara network — Admiral document origin confirmed as external courier; archivist — relationship deepened

faction_actions.action: The mounted scout reached a ridge overlooking the Twin Cities southern approach road before noon. He observed: the Dur Khadur camp still standing, no movement of troops, no caravan activity. He sent a drum-message back to the elder council: 'Camp stands. No war. No trade.' The elder council extended the caravan suspension another two days. Elder Mrak argued for permanent rerouting. The council did not vote but the argument has now been made formally. Cost: 1 gold (scout operations, drum network).
faction_actions.reason: The Gilgeth elders read stability through trade flow. No trade means instability regardless of the military picture. Elder Mrak's argument for permanent rerouting reflects a deeper organizational pressure: the opioid-dependent caravan crews are fraying under the suspension, and rerouting might break the addiction loop by disruption.
faction_actions.target: Scout — ridge observation complete; caravan suspension — extended two more days; Elder Mrak's rerouting proposal — formally on the table

faction_actions.action: Caeris visited Roderic again at dusk. The king did not wake. Caeris sat beside him for an hour and said nothing. On the way out he stopped in the corridor and told the head physician: 'If his condition changes — either direction — you come to me first, before anyone.' The physician agreed. Caeris returned to his office and left the proclamation in the archive unopened. He did not draft. He waited. Cost: 1 gold (physician arrangement), 2 influence (political positioning).
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is not paralyzed — he is calibrating. The legal scaffolding is being built. The physician is now his early warning. He is waiting for the Dur Khadur situation to resolve visibly before he makes any move that requires public credibility.
faction_actions.target: King Roderic — bedside visit, no response; head physician — first-alert arrangement made; proclamation — untouched

recent_events.text: The Dur Khadur unit received the Tidefall recall dispatch at midday. The commander read it, queried the Dark Council, and ordered rest but not withdrawal. The camp at four miles is standing. Caeris does not know the dispatch arrived. His second Hollow Accord letter sits unread on the admiralty desk.

Kessa Stonebraid examined the iron case's outer runes and reported two things: the runes resist opening from within, not from without — and something has been pressing against them for longer than the case has been in Lostfeld. Ironmaul doubled the garrison and cleared the yard. Ashveil is one day away.

Equipment is ready. Weighted descent lines, sealed lanterns, and a deep-water sailor named Briggs are prepared for a dawn dive. Rowen has tied the iron key to his own wrist and will descend personally. The fleet holds anchor.

The Admiral did not emerge today. Palace staff confirmed to the senior trade councillor: a sealed document of external origin was delivered under the study door on Day 16. The councillor now has this information and three quiet allies. The prophet prays in the corridor outside the private chamber.

Lythara finalized her clan council strategy: she will not defend — she will offer the assembled houses a share of leverage from Tidefall's internal crisis. She needs her Nythariel envoy's intelligence to arrive before dawn. Velorn sent private messages to two of her client houses last night.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Ironmaul vs Elder Ashveil (Entity Contact Suspected)
active_tensions.description: Ashveil arrives tomorrow. Kessa Stonebraid's rune examination has revealed the case is resisting pressure from within. Varek spoke of an entity listening from below. Ironmaul has doubled the garrison and cleared the yard. The Monastery's relay message warning Ashveil may or may not arrive before she does.

active_tensions.factions: Dur Khadur — Unit Holds Against Recall Order
active_tensions.description: The recall dispatch was received but the unit has not withdrawn. The Dark Council is deliberating. Tomorrow the Council's response reaches the unit commander. If the Council authorizes non-compliance, the situation transforms from diplomatic friction into a test of whether the Hollow Accord has any enforcement mechanism.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Lythara vs Velorn, Council Convenes Tomorrow
active_tensions.description: Velorn has five houses, a position statement, and a private message campaign targeting Lythara's client houses. Lythara has an offensive strategy that depends on intelligence she does not yet hold. The clan council opens tomorrow and no intermediary exists to slow it.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — The Sealed Document and the Admiral's Fitness
active_tensions.description: An external sealed document was delivered under the Admiral's door on Day 16. This is now known to three councillors. The prophet is praying outside the chamber. If the document's origin can be identified, the political consequences for the Admiral — and whoever sent it — will be severe.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
